Based on the AI model's prediction,
The fact that there is no checking account, due to its risk-mitigating effect, because it correlates with lower default probabilities, is known in the literature as a signal that decreases credit risk, and the model also identifies it as a factor that decreases risk. [1]
The fact that the loan duration is shorter, due to its established risk-enhancing pattern, because it aligns with lower default rates, is known in the literature as a signal that increases credit risk, but the model instead identifies it as a factor that decreases risk. [2]
The fact that the credit history shows critical accounts or other existing credits, due to its theoretical risk significance, because it aligns with lower default probabilities, is known in the literature as a signal that increases credit risk, but the model instead identifies it as a factor that decreases risk. [3]
Overall, this applicant is likely to have a low risk of default.
